display.purpose: Hero headline, big statements, statement codas, footer CTA — every oversized moment. clamp(40px, 8.4vw, 112px).

lead.purpose: Lead paragraph directly under a headline; capped ~62ch.

eyebrow.purpose: Uppercase section labels (real h2s), nav links, card metadata.

shadows.name: ambient-rest
shadows.purpose: Default soft shadow under card-level surfaces.

shadows.name: ambient-hover
shadows.purpose: Deeper shadow on hover, paired with border-brightening.

shadows.name: accent-glow
shadows.purpose: Live-status dot glow in About section; the only colored shadow (signature mint).

motion.name: reveal
motion.purpose: Scroll-reveal entrance (gated on html.js, disabled under prefers-reduced-motion).

components.name: Pill
components.description: Highest-status label per context (e.g. role title) — solid mint fill, near-black text.
components.css: .ds-pill { display: inline-block; background: #83D4C3; color: #0E1416; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; padding: 7px 14px; border-radius: 100px; }

components.name: Chip
components.description: Quieter secondary metadata tag on an inset surface.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-block; background: #26211A; color: #C9BCA6; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; padding: 7px 13px; border-radius: 100px; }

components.name: Case Study Card
components.description: Distilled two-column card: eyebrow (num · client · theme), title, italic question, CTA. No description paragraph, no chips. Whole card is one link.
components.html:
01MoxiWorks·Organizational Complexity

Designing Brand Governance for Enterprise Scale

How do you help thousands of agents stay on brand without manual processes?

View case study →
components.css: .ds-case-card { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1.05fr 0.95fr; background: #1A1610; border: 1px solid rgba(241,235,223,0.12); border-radius: 20px; overflow: hidden; box-shadow: 0 4px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.25); text-decoration: none; color: inherit; transition: border-color 0.4s, box-shadow 0.4s; } .ds-case-card:hover, .ds-case-card:focus-visible { border-color: rgba(241,235,223,0.26); box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.32); } .ds-case-card__body { padding: 48px 50px; } .ds-case-card__eyebrow { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.10em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #A1957F; margin-bottom: 22px; } .ds-case-card__num { color: #97F8E4; font-weight: 700; } .ds-case-card__client { color: #E7DFD2; } .ds-case-card__dot { color: #736959; } .ds-case-card__title { margin: 0 0 18px; font-family: 'Bricolage Grotesque', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: clamp(28px, 3.6vw, 46px); line-height: 1.1; letter-spacing: -0.03em; font-weight: 700; color: #F1EBDF; } .ds-case-card__q { margin: 0 0 18px; font-size: clamp(17px, 1.6vw, 21px); font-style: italic; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.6; color: #D8CFC0; } .ds-case-card__cta { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.10em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #A1957F; transition: color 0.3s; } .ds-case-card:hover .ds-case-card__cta { color: #F1EBDF; } .ds-case-card__media { background: #26211A; min-height: 420px; }

components.name: Footer CTA
components.description: The site's primary call to action: an oversized Alegreya mailto link. The CTA is display type; on hover only the arrow shifts mint.
components.css: .ds-footer-cta { display: block; font-family: 'Alegreya', Georgia, serif; font-size: clamp(2.5rem, 8.5vw, 8rem); line-height: 0.94; letter-spacing: -0.012em; font-weight: 800; color: #F1EBDF; text-decoration: none; } .ds-footer-cta__arrow { color: #97F8E4; transition: color 0.3s; } .ds-footer-cta:hover .ds-footer-cta__arrow { color: #83D4C3; }

components.name: Site Header Nav Link
components.description: Uppercase eyebrow-style sticky-header nav link with a 44px tap box.
components.css: .ds-nav-link { font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.10em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #A1957F; text-decoration: none; padding-block: 15px; margin-block: -15px; transition: color 0.3s; } .ds-nav-link:hover, .ds-nav-link:focus-visible { color: #F1EBDF; }

components.name: Mobile Nav Toggle
components.description: The only real <button>: a 44x44 hamburger shown below 600px; its two bars rotate into an X when expanded.
components.css: .ds-toggle { display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center; align-items: center; gap: 5px; width: 44px; height: 44px; padding: 0; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; } .ds-toggle__bar { display: block; width: 20px; height: 2px; background: #F1EBDF; transition: transform 0.3s, opacity 0.3s; } .ds-toggle[aria-expanded="true"] .ds-toggle__bar:first-child { transform: translateY(3.5px) rotate(45deg); } .ds-toggle[aria-expanded="true"] .ds-toggle__bar:last-child { transform: translateY(-3.5px) rotate(-45deg); }

narrative.overview: A warm near-black canvas (#15120D) holds the page; one signature mint accent (oklch(0.81 0.084 179), keyed to the hand-lettered TN wordmark) is the only thing allowed to call attention to itself. Everything else — type, chips, borders — recedes into a tight ramp of warm off-white to deep brown. Type is a serif/grotesque pairing on a real contrast axis: Alegreya (calligraphic, echoing the wordmark) for every oversized moment, Bricolage Grotesque for body and UI. The system speaks once, says something precise, and lets the case studies carry the rest of the argument. It rejects the generic AI-template look: no gradients, no glassmorphism, no interchangeable hero, no second accent.
keyCharacteristics: One accent color (signature mint), used sparingly, never diluted with a second hue
Warm near-black base instead of true black or cool gray
A serif/grotesque pairing on a real contrast axis — Alegreya display, Bricolage Grotesque body/UI
Flat-by-default surfaces with tonal layering, accented by soft ambient shadows for depth
Borders + soft shadow signal interactivity on hover, not color shifts
Progressive enhancement: html.js-gated reveals, a real-content noscript fallback, and prefers-reduced-motion respected

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Signature mint is the only saturated color in the system. If a new UI need calls for 'another color,' the answer is a neutral tone from the existing ramp, not a second hue.

rules.name: The Display Ceiling Rule
rules.body: Hero display type caps at 112px (7rem), not 118px: at 118px the longest line ('Complexity is inevitable.') renders ~1183px and overflows the 1180px container by 3px. Paired with an 8.4vw fluid term it stays one line from ~500px up; phones wrap to 3 lines by design.

rules.name: The Serif-For-Scale Rule
rules.body: Alegreya appears only at display scale (hero, statements, footer CTA). Body, UI, labels, and anything section-size or smaller stay Bricolage. The two never trade roles.

rules.name: The Diffuse-Not-Dark Rule
rules.body: Shadows stay low-opacity and large-radius (soft ambient glow), never small-radius/high-opacity '2014 app' drop shadows. If a shadow has a visible hard edge, the blur radius is too small and the opacity is too high.

dos: Do keep signature mint (oklch(0.81 0.084 179), text variant oklch(0.91 0.097 179)) as the only saturated accent on any screen; everything else stays within the warm-neutral ramp.
Do use the Ambient Rest / Ambient Hover shadow pair (0 4px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.25) → 0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.32)) for any new card-level surface, paired with the existing border-brightening hover.
Do step through the neutral surface ramp (Warm Pitch → Card Brown → Inset Brown) to convey containment/depth before reaching for a new color.
Do cap hero display type at 112px (7rem) and keep line-height tight (0.94); don't raise the cap or the line overflows the container.
Do keep the type system to the two committed families — Alegreya (display) and Bricolage Grotesque (body/UI) — pairing on a serif-vs-grotesque contrast axis.
Do write copy the way a senior designer talks to a peer — specific and confident, not marketing-voice.
donts: Don't introduce a second saturated accent color (no purple, no magenta, no multi-color gradient) — this breaks the One Accent Rule and reads as generic AI-template styling.
Don't use purple gradients, glassmorphism, interchangeable stock-photo hero sections, or other generic SaaS-landing-page clichés — explicit anti-references from PRODUCT.md.
Don't use hard, small-radius drop shadows (the '2014 app' look).
Don't add a third type family or swap the display serif for a reflex default (Fraunces, Playfair, Cormorant, Recoleta). Alegreya is the committed choice.

Don't let muted/faint text fall below 4.5:1. text-faint (#736959) is decorative-only (separator dots, placeholder icons); never use it for readable copy. On lighter surfaces (bg-inset) use text-muted.
